Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (Xi Jinping; born: 15 June, 1953) is the President of China and the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China. He is also the Chairman of China’s Central Military Commission and is well known as the leader of the Communist Party of China. He is the son of Xi Zhongxun and began his political career in Fujian province.
Xi Jinping later served as the party chief of Zhejiang province. He entered higher politics by taking responsibility as the party chief of Shanghai and became well known as the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China.
He is known for his strong stance against corruption and for liberal policies regarding political and market economic reforms. His market policies and politics have been established within the Communist Party of China.
As the President of China, Xi Jinping is known for organizing efforts against corruption and for liberal policies regarding political reforms. He is well known as the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China and, as the President of China, is known for organizing efforts against corruption and for liberal policies regarding political reforms.
